Description: The Afador is a cross between the elegant  Afghan Hound  and friendly  Labrador Retriever  dog breeds. Loyal, energetic, and affectionate, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents. These dogs possess an exceptional combination of traits that make them outstanding companions. Afadors can exhibit a wide range of characteristics inherited from their parent breeds. Some Afadors have the Afghan Hound’s long, flowing coat, while others display the Labrador Retriever’s shorter, dense coat. Additionally, their coat colors can vary. They may include black, brown, cream, or white, sometimes with beautiful patterns or markings. Afadors thrive on regular exercise as an active breed. Engaging their agile minds and providing opportunities for physical exertion through daily walks, playtime, and interactive games are essential. To that end, these activities prevent boredom and help curb potentially destructive behaviors. When considering an Afador, it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you decide to purchase an Afador puppy, it’s crucial to choose a  reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable Afador breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures that you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

The Affenhuahua is a mixed breed dog–a cross between the  Chihuahua  and  Affenpinscher  dog breeds. Petite, sassy, and highly energetic, these pups inherited some of the best traits from both of their parents. Affenhuahuas are also sometimes called Affen Chi. Despite their status as a designer breed, you can find these mixed breed dogs in shelters and rescues, so adopt! Don’t shop! These compact, cute pups make great apartment dogs for active city dwellers, though they can also thrive in small or single-person households. They can also be a bit territorial and yappy. If you want an energetic, sassy dog who will keep you on your toes, alert you to any dangers, and love you unconditionally, the Affenhuahua may be the right dog for you! DogTime recommends  this carrier for traveling  with your small Affenhuahua.  You should also  pick up this dog water bottle  for any outdoor adventures you have with your pup! See below for all mixed dog breed traits and facts about Affenhuahuas!

Height: 9 to 11 inches tall at the shoulder
Description: The Affenpinscher is a small toy breed from Germany, originally bred for pest control in homes, stables, and shops. While highly effective at catching mice, they later became popular as companion pets. A terrier-like breed, the Affenpinscher’s name reflects its distinctive appearance: “Affe” means monkey in German, and “Pinscher” means terrier. However, their charming looks are just one of the many traits that make this breed a beloved companion. Beyond their appearance, the Affenpinscher is known for its courageous, feisty, outgoing, funny, and intelligent temperament. With mischievous personalities, they love getting into trouble and aren’t afraid to stand up to larger dogs, often fighting back if they feel threatened. While they form strong bonds with their humans, Affenpinschers typically prefer the company of adults, as they don’t tolerate rough play or being held on a lap for extended periods. However, they are loving, loyal, and protective dogs, making them wonderful additions to any family. When considering an Affenpinscher, it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you decide to purchase an Affenpinscher puppy, it’s crucial to choose a  reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable Affenpinscher breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures that you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

Height: 24 to 26 inches tall at the shoulder
Description: The Afghan Hound is a medium-to-large-sized hound breed first bred by the Bedouin people of Afghanistan to hunt gazelle and other small game in the harsh terrain of the Afghan mountains. Later, Afghan Hounds were also used as war dogs. Of course, this beloved breed found their way into historical art and literature .  recognized by their unique aesthetic, silky fur, and piercing eyes, they’re undeniably striking. Beyond appearance, Afghan Hounds are also incredibly intelligent and highly trainable. An ancient  dog breed  with a history dating back thousands of years, Afghan Hounds have a reputation for being one of the most expensive dog breeds in the world. If you are considering bringing an Afghan Hound into your home, be sure to do your research and find a reputable breeder. While they are wonderful dogs, they require an owner who can give them the proper training and attention. They are not the best choice for apartment living, as they need plenty of space to run and play. When considering an Afghan Hound, it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you decide to purchase an Afghan Hound puppy, choosing a reputable breeder is crucial. Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable Afghan Hound breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

Height: 21 to 23 inches tall at the shoulder
Description: The Airedale Terrier, commonly called the “King of Terriers,” is the largest of all terrier breeds. Airedales are loyal and loving companions, and they make great family pets. They possesses athleticism and are highly trainable, leading them to excel in a variety of dog sports, including obedience, agility, and tracking. Airedales originated in the Aire Valley of Yorkshire, England, in the mid-19th century. They were originally bred to hunt otters and other vermin but quickly became popular as working dogs and show dogs. Airedales served in World War I and II as messenger, guard, and ambulance dogs. If you are looking for a loyal, intelligent, and versatile dog, the Airedale Terrier may be the perfect breed for you. They are a great choice for active families who are looking for a dog to share their adventures. However, if you are considering getting an  Airedale Terrier puppy , be sure to do your research and find a  reputable breeder  of Airedale Terrier puppies. Airedales are a great breed, but they require a lot of care and attention. With proper care, Airedales can make wonderful companions for many years to come.

The Akbash is a  large working breed first used as a livestock guardian in the plains and mountains of Turkey. Sometimes confused with the  Great Pyrenees , the Akbash also commands attention with their striking  white coat and confident demeanor. Highly formidable, Akbash dogs actively excel as  protectors of flocks and herds , fearlessly protecting their charges from predators. Their vigilant nature makes them an integral part of farm life. With an acute awareness of their surroundings, Akbash dogs constantly monitor the perimeter, deterring potential threats with their authoritative presence. Of course, this protective and independent temperament means the Akbash breed also makes a watchful companion. They are gentle and protective towards their family but can also be suspicious of strangers .  They are independent thinkers who must be trained with a firm but gentle hand. Akbash dogs are brave and courageous and will not hesitate to defend their family or flock from danger. They are also very loyal and enjoy spending time with their loved ones. It’s always advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters. However, if you’re searching Akbash puppies, it’s crucial to choose  a reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable breeders prioritize the health and temperament of puppies. They also conduct necessary health screenings and provide a nurturing environment for the dogs. This active approach ensures you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

Height: 24 inches to 28 tall at the shoulder
Description: The Akita, also known as the Japanese Akita, the Akita Inu, or the Japanese Akita Inu, is a large-sized working breed from Japan. They are known for their loyalty, courage, and independence. They are typically white, brown, or brindle with a thick, double coat that is weather-resistant. Originally bred for hunting large game such as bears and boars, Akitas were also used as guard dogs and were prized for their loyalty and courage. Two Akita varieties exist — the Akita Inu (Akita or Japanese Akita) and the  American Akita . There are ongoing debates over whether the two are separate breeds or not, although the American Akita, a larger and more muscular version of the breed, comes in a wider variety of colors. The American variety evolved in the United States with a focus on strength and size. American Akitas may also exhibit a more outgoing and protective temperament. Both breeds share physical traits such as a powerful build, double coat, and curled tail, but their temperaments and regional influences contribute to unique qualities that prospective owners should consider based on their preferences and lifestyle. When considering an Akita dog, it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you’re on the search for Akita puppies, it’s crucial to choose a  reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures that you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

The Akita Chow is a mixed breed dog–a cross between the  Akita  and  Chow Chow  dog breeds. Large, independent, and loyal, these pups inherited some of the best traits from their parents. The Akita Chow can also be called  Chakita . Despite their unfortunate status as a designer breed, you may find these mixed breed pups in shelters and breed-specific rescues, so remember to adopt! Don’t shop! Akita Chows are quiet, and while they may not be overly affectionate, they are incredibly protective and loyal. Expert dog parents recommended! You will have your work cut out for you with training, as they’re known for being willful and can  even be obstinate . The Akita Chow would make a great addition for an active family with older children or in a single person home, so long as they get plenty of exercise and will not be left alone for long periods. They wouldn’t be suitable for an apartment but would love a house with a yard so they have plenty of room to stretch their legs. You’ll also need to invest a fair amount of time into their training. Akita Chows would make great walking, running, or hiking companions. This is a highly intelligent dog. Keep them stimulated and keep them content! When considering an Akita Chow, it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you decide to purchase an  Akita Chow puppy , it’s crucial to choose a  reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable Akita Chow breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures that you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

The Akita Pit is a mixed breed dog–a cross between the  Akita  and  American Pit Bull Terrier  dog breeds. Intelligent, protective, and loyal, these pups inherited some of the best traits from both of their parents. Akita Pits are also sometimes called Pit Akita or Akita Pit Bull. Despite their unfortunate status as a designer breed, you can find these mixed breed dogs in shelters and rescues, especially given how many Pit Bull mixes are in shelters. So remember to adopt! Don’t shop! These adorable dogs are fairly adaptable and can thrive in smaller homes with active urban dwellers, though they’re best suited to larger homes or families. If you want a protective dog who will test your boundaries every once in a while, alert you to any dangers, and love you unconditionally, then the Akita Pit may be the right dog for you! DogTime recommends  this dog bed to give a good night’s sleep  to your medium-sized Akita Pit. You should also  pick up this dog brush and massager  for your short-haired pup! See below for all mixed dog breed traits and facts about Akita Pits!

An Akita Shepherd is a mixed breed dog–a cross between the  Akita  and  German Shepherd Dog  breeds. Stoic, loyal, and hard-working, these pups inherited some of the best traits from both of their parents. The Akita Shepherd is sometimes also called a Shepkita. Despite their unfortunate status as a designer breed, you may find these mixed breed dogs in shelters and breed specific rescues, so remember to adopt! Don’t shop! These adorable mixed breed dogs make great apartment pups for active urban dwellers, though as a larger dog, they are best suited to houses or homes with more space. They are quiet but alert, which also makes them excellent watchdogs for both singles and families. If you want a stoic, loyal dog who’s eager to please and will alert you of any potential danger, the Akita Shepherd might be the right dog for you. DogTime recommends  this big, spacious crate  to give your big Akita Shepherd a place to rest and relax. You should also  pick up this dog brush and massager  for your long-haired pup! See below for all mixed dog breed traits and facts about Akita Shepherds!

Small, smart, and energetic, the Alaskan Klee Kai is a relatively new breed. These little dogs have a  wolf-like appearance  and are sometimes mistaken for miniature Husky. Even the name “Klee Kai” comes from an Inuit term meaning “small dog.” While these little dogs are small, weighing only 16 to 22 pounds, they pack a serious punch of personality. Developed in the late 20th century, this small companion dog was bred from a distinctive mix of northern and Spitz breeds to create a compact breed that resembles the  Siberian Husky  and  Alaskan Malamute  in appearance but is much smaller in size. While this adorable dog may resemble a miniature Husky, they have some key differences, especially regarding temperament and purpose. These differences distinguish this unique breed from them from their Husky ancestors of the north. This breed is more suited to the life of a companion animal  rather than that of a working dog . However, the Alaskan Klee Kai shares the high energy of the Siberian Husky and demands plenty of exercise. In that regard, calling this pup a miniature Husky isn’t too far off! Still, these are completely separate breeds. They also tend to be shy around strangers and are prone to expressing their emotions with whines and barks. An inexperienced pet parent would find it difficult to take on the challenge of caring for this unique breed. This may be worth noting for anyone considering adding an  Alaskan Klee Kai puppy  to their home. But for an adopter who keeps up with training and physical activity, this breed is sweet, loyal, and happy to shower loved ones with affection. When considering an Alaskan Klee Kai, it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. Despite the growing trend of the “Mini Husky”, these dogs still end up in shelters. However, if you decide to purchase an Alaskan Klee Kai puppy, it’s crucial to choose a  reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable Alaskan Klee Kai breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures that you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

Height: 23 to 25 inches tall at the shoulder
Description: The Alaskan Malamute, bold, friendly, and hardworking, originates from the rugged landscapes of Alaska. With strength, endurance, and unwavering loyalty, these large fluffy dogs were an asset to indigenous people in the north. Belonging to the Mahlemuts of Alaska, Malamutes hold a significant place in the history of the Arctic. They thrived in cold climates, thanks to their thick coat. The double coat also provides insulation against freezing temperatures and comes in various colors. This includes shades of gray, black, white, and copper. It probably goes without saying, but  these pups shed a lot . Malamutes are large, robust dogs designed for endurance. Initially bred to  pull heavy sleds  over extended distances, this breed assisted in hauling supplies and transportation tasks. This strong work ethic remains intrinsic, making regular exercise and  mental stimulation  vital to their well-being. Neglecting these needs can lead to a mischievous Malamute, so engaging activities and frequent outdoor adventures are imperative. When considering an Alaskan Malamute, it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you decide to purchase an Alaskan Malamute puppy, choosing a reputable breeder is crucial. Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs.  Reputable Alaskan Malamute breeders  prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

The American Bulldog is a medium-to-large-sized breed of dog that originated in the United States. They are known for their stocky build, muscular body, and distinctive “bulldog” head. Friendly, loyal, and affectionate, these dogs make wonderful family pets. They are also known for being good watchdogs and are protective of their families. While the American Bulldog is stocky and muscular, they are also agile. They were first bred for chasing down stray cattle and helping with farm work. The American Bulldog personality is characterized by intelligence, affection, and loyalty, which make them great family dogs. However, they also have high exercise needs and require an experienced pet parent. They can vary in appearance, as there are multiple types. This includes the Bully or Classic type, also known as the Johnson type, the Standard or Performance type, also called the Scott type, and hybrids of the two. It’s important to remember that these dogs take a good amount of work. This may be worth keeping in mind if you’re thinking of adding an  American Bulldog puppy  to your home. As always, early socialization is key! When considering an American Bulldog, it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you’re on the search for American Bulldog puppies and decide to purchase, it’s crucial to choose a  reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable American Bulldog breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures that you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

Height: 23 to 26 inches tall at the shoulder
Description: The American English Coonhound, also known as the Redtick Coonhound, is a large-sized Hound breed and a descendant of the  English Foxhound . One of six Coonhound dog breeds, Redtick Coonhounds are known for their speed, endurance, and loud voice in the field. The American English Coonhound is also beloved as a sporting and household companion. It’s also worth noting that these pups will bark and bay at home. This may make them a poor choice if you’ve got nearby neighbors. They are known for their intelligent, energetic, affectionate, and lively temperaments. Like the other Coonhound breeds, the  Black and Tan Coonhound , the  Redbone Coonhound ,  the American English Coonhound , the  Bluetick Coonhound , and the  Plott Coonhound , these dogs are loving, intelligent, easy to train, and fairly easy to groom. However, novice pet parents should beware. They also need firm, consistent training and mental and physical activity. If you can’t keep up with seemingly endless walks and play sessions, you may want to consider a less energetic breed. But if you can provide the exercise they need, a secure yard for them to roam, and patient training, you’ll have an adoring furry family member. When considering a Coonhound, it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you decide to purchase an American English Coonhound puppy, it’s crucial to choose a  reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable American English Coonhound breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs. Additionally, they conduct necessary health screenings and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

Height: 15 to 19 inches tall at the shoulder
Description: The American Eskimo Dog, affectionately called the “Eskie,” is a friendly companion breed from Germany. A German Spitz breed, these dogs lost popularity following World War II. In a marketing move, they were renamed the American Eskimo Dog, likely due to their similar appearance to the  Samoyed , another fluffy white breed originating in Siberia who later made their way to the Arctic regions and Alaska. You can usually spot Eskie dogs by their small size, fluffy white coats, and curly tails. While the American Eskimo Dog’s coat is usually pure white, they can also have biscuit-colored markings. Regarding temperament, American Eskimo Dogs are alert, intelligent, social, and affectionate. Eskies are also known for their eagerness to please their owners, a trait that separates them from other northern working breeds. They are less independent than Samoyeds and  Huskies , which can make them more trainable. They excel in obedience and agility activities. Alert and affectionate, these pups make wonderful companions. It’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you decide to purchase, choosing a reputable breeder is crucial. Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable  American Eskimo Dog breeders  prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

Height: 21 to 25 inches tall at the shoulder
Description: The American Foxhound, a breed known for its grace and endurance, originated in the United States. Bred primarily for  hunting , the foxhound possesses a keen sense of smell and boundless energy, making them exceptional hunters and tracking companions.. They are also friendly, affectionate, and gentle, making them wonderful family dogs. American Foxhounds are medium to large in size, standing between 21 and 25 inches at the shoulder and weighing around 65 to 75 pounds. The Foxhound’s sleek and athletic build enables them to excel in chasing prey over vast distances with remarkable speed and agility. Regular brushing helps keep their short, dense coat clean and healthy. In the field, American Foxhounds are actively alert and determined, tracking scents with utmost precision. To that end, their inherent hunting instincts drive them to be persistent and tenacious, traits that make them prized among hunters and farmers. To keep them  mentally stimulated  and physically satisfied, these active dogs require regular exercise. For future pet parents considering an  American Foxhound puppy , walks and active play sessions are essential to maintain their overall well-being and prevent behavioral issues that may arise from pent-up energy. Additionally, proper training is vital for this breed, as their hunting instincts may lead to chasing behavior if not appropriately channeled. Early  socialization  and strong leadership are key.

The American Hairless Terrier is a small to medium-sized terrier breed and the only hairless dog indigenous to the United States. The breed’s creation was a happy accident, having been developed from naturally hairless Rat Terriers. Today, the breed is known as an active companion dog and an especially great choice for allergy sufferers. Like many terrier breeds, the American Hairless Terrier temperament is curious, spunky, and intelligent. They can thrive in larger family homes with kids or active urban dwellers in apartments or condos. If you’re looking for a small dog who enjoys snuggling, being a goof, and alerting you to potential dangers, this spunky pup just might be the dog for you! When considering an American Hairless Terrier, it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you decide to purchase an American Hairless Terrier puppy, it’s crucial to choose a  reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

The American Leopard Hound, also known as the American Leopard Cur, is an intelligent and hardy  dog  that boasts a rich history intertwined with the arrival of Spanish conquistadors. These medium to large-sized hounds trace their lineage back to various European breeds, resulting in a versatile  hunting dog  prized for their keen tracking abilities and athletic prowess. Today, the American Leopard Hound remains a relatively rare breed, captivating hearts with their distinctive spotted coat, energetic spirit, and unwavering loyalty. Despite their hunting background, American Leopard Hounds can adapt to various living situations with  proper training  and  exercise . Their intelligence, coupled with a strong desire to please, makes them trainable companions for active families seeking a loyal and adventurous canine friend. When considering an  American Leopard Hound puppy , it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you decide to purchase, it’s crucial to choose  a reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable American Leopard Hound puppy breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures that you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

Height: 17 to 19 inches tall at the shoulder
Description: The American Pit Bull Terrier is a muscular, short-coated companion dog from a long line of working breed ancestors traced back to England. Originally bred to “bait” bulls in the fighting pits, the breed evolved into all-around farm dogs and later moved into the house to become “nanny dogs” because they were so gentle around children. The American Pit Bull Terrier’s name is often shortened to Pit Bull; however, “Pit Bull” is not an official breed but an umbrella term that refers to a group of breeds with similar physical characteristics. There are different types of Pit Bulls, the five most common being the American Pit Bull Terrier, the  American Staffordshire Terrier , the  Staffordshire Bull Terrier , the  Bull Terrier , and the  American Bully . Pit Bull Terriers have a reputation for being aggressive, largely due to their history of being used for dog fighting. However, Pit Bulls are no more aggressive than any other dog breed. The American Temperament Test Society has ranked them as the fourth most gentle breed.  Pit Bulls  are often the target of breed discrimination and are banned in some cities and towns. They are often denied insurance coverage. This is unfortunate, as Pit Bulls are loving and loyal dogs that make great family pets. When considering an American Pit Bull Terrier, it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. Shelters and rescues are beyond capacity for many dogs, especially American Pit Bull Terriers and Pitbull Mixes. You can easily find American Pit Bull Terrier puppies at a shelter. However, if you decide to purchase, it’s crucial to choose a  reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable Pit Bull Terrier breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures that you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

The American Pugabull is a mixed breed dog — a cross between the  American Bull Dog  and  Pug  dog breeds. Medium in size, alert, and loyal, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents. American Pugabulls are also called Bull Pugs. Despite their unfortunate status as a designer breed, you may find these mixed breed dogs in shelters and rescues, so remember to adopt! Don’t shop! These adorable pups fit in nicely with big families and also make great apartment dogs for active urban dwellers They’re loyal and playful yet very protective — if you’re a family member, this pup will not hesitate to protect you. They will alert to any suspicious characters who approach your door. They will also alert you to any non suspicious characters. Socialize them early with people and animals. Read on and find out if the American Pugabull is the right dog for you! See below for all American Pugabull facts and mixed dog breed characteristics!

The American Staffordshire Terrier, also known as the AmStaff, is a muscular, short-coated breed from the United States. The breed was developed in the 1800s when the Staffordshire Bull Terrier was brought to America and later bred as a larger version. In time, this breed was ultimately given distinction from the  Staffordshire Bull Terrier . The American Staffordshire Terrier may be somewhat imposing in appearance, but they’re known for their loving temperament and are very affectionate with human family members, particularly children. They are also intelligent and eager to please, which makes them highly trainable. To that end, their intelligence means they need adequate mental stimulation. If they don’t get it, they’ll put those strong jaws to use and chew anything out of boredom. Socialization is also important to help the breed overcome their natural standoffish tendencies. This breed loves having a job to do, and they do well in athletic competitions, therapy work, and as a service animal. The American Staffordshire Terrier can be a loyal cuddle bug and family companion, as well as a competent working dog. If you’re thinking of bringing an American Staffordshire Terrier puppy home, be sure to check shelters first. Unfortunately, many American Staffy dogs and mixes are in desperate need of loving homes. You can very likely find one at a shelter near you. If you decide to purchase an adult or American Staffy puppy, it’s crucial to choose a  reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable American Staffordshire Terrier breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures that you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

The American Water Spaniel, a distinct and versatile breed native to the United States, is prized for its exceptional hunting and retrieving abilities, especially in waterfowl environments. Medium-sized and compact, this breed is known for its curly or wavy brown coat and expressive eyes. With a friendly and eager-to-please disposition, American Water Spaniels make superb family pets and devoted companions. Intelligent and adaptabe, these charming pups excel in various roles, from hunting to agility competitions. This breed’s enduring popularity is a testament to its affectionate personality and its knack for adapting to both rural and urban living environments, provided it receives the exercise and mental stimulation it craves. The American Water Spaniel was bred to be an all-around  hunting dog . Specializing in waterfowl, these skilled swimmers will happily retrieve from small boats, protected by their water-resistant double coats. This  breed  has the high energy of a dog born to chase and retrieve game, but given enough exercise, they can also make great family companions. If you can keep up and provide plenty of space to run, then this may be the breed for you! When considering an American Water Spaniel, it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you’re on the search for  American Water Spaniel puppies  and decide to purchase, it’s crucial to choose a  reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable American Water Spaniel breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures that you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

The Anatolian Shepherd Dog is a native of Turkey, where they were bred as a shepherd’s companion and  livestock guardian . They were created with specific traits to resemble the size and color of livestock they defended so predators wouldn’t detect them among the flock. Sometimes called the Anatolian Karabash Dog, they’re a fiercely loyal guard dog and a large, impressive  dog breed , frequently weighing 120 to 150 pounds at maturity. Novice pet parents beware. Not only are dogs of this breed big, but they’re generally known to be stubborn. Anatolian Shepherd Dogs require firm, consistent trainers with experience handling dogs. Also, be ready to clean up all the fur they shed throughout the year. This may be worth considering for any future pet parents thinking of adding an  Anatolian Shepherd puppy  to their home. With patience and consistency, a well-trained and socialized Anatolian Shepherd is a friend and guardian for anyone they consider to be their “flock”, which includes the human members of their family. When considering an Anatolian Shepherd, it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you decide to purchase an Anatolian Shepherd puppy, it’s crucial to choose a  reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable Anatolian Shepherd breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures that you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

Height: 19 to 22 inches tall at the shoulder
Description: The Appenzeller Sennenhunde, also known as the Appenzeller Mountain Dog, is a medium-sized working breed that originated as an all-around farm dog that would herd livestock, guard the farm, and pull carts in their native Switzerland. Today’s Appenzeller still has the energy, smarts, and self-confidence that makes for valuable working dogs — but they’re anything but low-maintenance. Dogs of this breed need lots of exercise, training, and a job to do. Apartment dwellers beware! These dogs need space to run and play. But families who can provide the mental and physical stimulation these dogs need will be rewarded with an affectionate, loyal companion. They’ll even adore kids; although, they may fall into some herding habits without proper training. Socialize and teach your dog early, and you’ll have a loving, furry family member! When considering an Appenzeller Sennenhunde, it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you decide to purchase an Appenzeller Sennenhunde puppy, it’s crucial to choose a  reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable Appenzeller Sennenhunde breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures that you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

The Auggie is an adorable and spirited hybrid who takes some of the most desirable traits from their  Australian Shepherd  and  Corgi  parents and combines them into one fun-loving, portable package. With a compact yet sturdy build, these pups typically stand 10-15 inches tall at the shoulder once fully grown and weigh 15-35 pounds. Their coats can vary, with some exhibiting the longer, thicker fur of the Aussie parent while others have the Corgi’s shorter but still dense double coat. Coat colors range widely too, including red merle, blue merle, black, and tan points. Despite their small size, Auggies are active  herding dogs  at heart who need plenty of daily exercise and  mental stimulation . They inherit the boundless energy and work ethic of the Mini Aussie along with the Corgi’s exceptional intelligence and driving herding instincts. This makes for an energetic, smart companion that excels at canine sports like agility and obedience. With proper training and  socialization  from an early age, Auggies can make excellent family dogs that do well with children. When considering  an Auggie puppy , it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you decide to purchase, it’s crucial to choose  a reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable Auggie puppy breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures that you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

The Aussiedoodle, also called the Aussiepoo, is a popular cross between an  Australian Shepherd  and a  Poodle . Fans of the breed love these pups for their intelligence, friendly disposition, and striking appearance. Typically, the Aussiepoo inherits the Poodle’s allergy-friendly coat, which is low-shedding and comes in various colors. As with any hypoallergenic dog breed, the possibility of shedding and allergic reactions to those with sensitivities is still possible. Aussiedoodles come in several sizes — toy, mini, and standard — depending on the  Poodle parent  used in the breeding. The Aussiedoodle temperament is characterized by a playful, friendly, and active personality. Because of their intelligence and adaptability, they learn quickly and are highly trainable. They are often described as curious and eager to please, making them great companions suitable for various dog sports and activities. However, they are energetic and require regular exercise and mental stimulation to stay happy and healthy. Aussiedoodles are considered high-maintenance due to their high energy levels and the required upkeep on their coats. They may not be the ideal breed choice for novice or first-time pet parents. Aussiedoodle coats can range from straight to wavy or curly, often influenced by the Poodle parent’s genes. Furthermore, they are usually hypoallergenic and low-shedding, making Aussiedoodles appealing to allergy-sensitive owners. Their color spectrum is equally impressive, showcasing black, white, cream, gray, brown, apricot, and red. Some display merle patterns, inherited from the Australian Shepherd parent, featuring a marbled mix of colors with unique, often blue or bi-colored, eyes. Other common patterns include parti (two-tone), phantom (tan markings similar to a Doberman), and solid colors. Full grown Aussiedoodles come in various sizes, dictated by whether the Poodle parent was standard, miniature, or toy. The size of a full-grown Aussiedoodle can vary widely depending on the size of their Poodle parent (toy, miniature, or standard). Toy Aussiedoodles are the smallest, typically weighing 10-20 pounds and standing around 10-12 inches at the shoulder. They are a result of breeding a Toy Poodle with a smaller Australian Shepherd. Miniature Aussiedoodles are slightly larger, weighing 15-35 pounds and standing 12-18 inches tall. These are bred from a Miniature Poodle and an Australian Shepherd. The largest variation is the Standard Aussiedoodle, which weighs between 40-70 pounds and stands 19-24 inches tall at the shoulder, coming from a Standard Poodle parent. Aussiedoodles are known for their friendly, intelligent, and outgoing temperament. They inherit the Australian Shepherd’s energetic and playful nature combined with the Poodle’s sharp intelligence and eagerness to please. This combination makes them highly trainable and responsive to commands, excelling in activities like obedience, agility, and even therapy work. Aussiedoodles are social dogs that thrive on human interaction and often form strong bonds with their families. They are great with children and other pets, showcasing a gentle and patient demeanor when well-socialized. However, their high energy levels and intelligence mean they require ample exercise and mental stimulation to prevent boredom, which can lead to undesirable behaviors. Aussiedoodles have many appealing qualities, but they also come with challenges that potential owners should consider. On the plus side, they are intelligent, affectionate, and highly trainable, making them excellent companions for families, singles, and active individuals. Their friendly and playful nature, combined with their generally low-shedding and hypoallergenic coats, makes them a great choice for households with allergies. They are also social and get along well with children and other pets. Prospective pet parents should know that Aussiedoodles require a significant commitment of time and energy. Their high intelligence and energy levels mean they need regular physical exercise and mental stimulation to prevent boredom, which can lead to destructive behaviors. Grooming can also be demanding, particularly for those with curly coats that require frequent brushing and trims. Additionally, their need for companionship means they can develop separation anxiety if left alone for long periods. Overall, Aussiedoodles are loving, versatile dogs but thrive best with attentive, active owners who can meet their needs. Early socialization and positive reinforcement training are crucial due to their intelligence and potential stubbornness. Of course, their intelligence allows them to pick up commands quickly, so early training is key to establishing good behavior from the start. Use praise, treats, and affection to reward desired behavior. Aussiedoodles respond well to positive reinforcement, and this approach will keep them motivated and engaged. As with all dogs, it’s important to keep up your Australian Shepherd Poodle mix’s regular veterinary checkups to detect any health concerns early. Your vet can help you develop a care routine that will keep your dog healthy. A couple of walks every day, totaling around 45 minutes, should do the trick to keep your Aussiedoodle happy and healthy. However, due to their remarkable intelligence, it’s of great benefit to incorporate obedience tasks into the exercise mix to keep them interested and mentally stimulated. The Aussiedoodle’s coat comes in an array of colors, patterns, and textures depending on their genetic makeup. The coats usually incorporate a mix of black, white, gray, and brown and are typically somewhere between medium and long in terms of length. It is often described as soft to the touch and wavy. Like other Poodle mixes, they don’t typically shed much and are usually considered hypoallergenic. Remember that this does not mean they do not cause allergies, only that they’re less likely to do so. Keep up with flea and tick prevention treatments, especially if your Aussiedoodle spends much time outdoors. Regular grooming allows you to spot parasites early. Getting your Aussiedoodle accustomed to grooming procedures from a young age makes the process easier and more enjoyable for both of you. Handle their paws frequently, examine their mouth and ears, and reward them for good behavior during grooming sessions. This positive foundation sets the stage for stress-free veterinary exams and handling throughout their lives. An ideal Aussiedoodle diet should be formulated for a medium breed with medium energy. Aussiedoodles need to stick to a healthy diet, as overeating can cause  weight gain  and associated health problems, especially if adequate exercise isn’t offered. As with all dogs, the Aussiedoodle’s dietary needs will change from  puppyhood  to  adulthood  and will continue to change into their  senior years . You should ask your veterinarian for recommendations about your Aussiedoodle’s diet, as there is far too much variation among individual dogs–including weight, energy, and health–to make a specific recommendation. Aussiedoodles are known for their excellent compatibility with families, thanks to their friendly, affectionate, and social nature. They form strong bonds with their family members and are typically very loyal, making them great companions for children, adults, and even other pets. Their playful and energetic personality means they enjoy participating in family activities, whether it’s running around the yard, playing fetch, or simply lounging together. Due to their high intelligence, they can be easily trained to interact well with children, learn boundaries and how to be gentle with younger family members. Aussiedoodles thrive in environments where they receive plenty of attention and affection, making them ideal for families looking for an interactive and loving dog. However, due to their active and social nature, Aussiedoodles require a lot of mental and physical stimulation to stay happy and avoid behavior issues. Families that can provide regular exercise, interactive play, and consistent training will find the Aussiedoodle a highly rewarding addition. These dogs do best in homes where they are not left alone for long periods, as they can develop separation anxiety. They also tend to be good with other pets, especially if properly socialized from a young age. The Aussiedoodle breed is predisposed to some of the same conditions the Australian Shepherd and Poodle face. While most are generally healthy, some may be prone to a few health issues, so it is important to maintain good care and regular veterinary checkups. Some of the most common health ailments Aussiedoodles suffer from include: Finding a breed-specific rescue for Aussiedoodles may be hard because they are a mixed breed. However, you may want to try Australian Shepherd or Poodle breed-specific rescues, as they often care for mixes. Here are some rescues you can try: The price of Aussiedoodle puppies typically ranges between $2,500 and $4,500 when purchased from a reputable breeder. While some breeders may offer Aussiedoodle puppies at much lower prices, this should be considered a warning sign. Unless the puppy is coming from a verified rescue and is more affordable for that reason, avoid deals that seem too good to be true. Unfortunately, many backyard breeders and puppy mills exploit the popularity of this breed, selling puppies for profit without regard for humane breeding practices. This increases the risk of ending up with a puppy suffering from illnesses or hereditary conditions. If you choose to purchase an Aussiedoodle or Mini Aussiedoodles puppy, always find a reputable breeder.

The Aussiepom is a lively hybrid breed that blends the intelligent and tireless  Australian Shepherd  with the spirited and portable  Pomeranian . These playful canines have a plush coat and a graceful frame, making them an ideal choice for active families and individuals alike. Due to their friendly natures and eager-to-please attitudes, Aussiepoms have quickly become a favorite among dog lovers. These clever companions thrive on  positive reinforcement training  and excel in various activities, from agility trials to therapy work. Early socialization is key to nurturing their outgoing personalities, ensuring they grow into well-rounded companions. They have a moderate size, and Aussiepoms possess an alert and confident demeanor, making them excellent watchdogs for their devoted owners. When considering  an Aussiepom puppy , it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you decide to purchase, it’s crucial to choose  a reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable Aussiepom breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures that you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

Height: 17 to 20 inches tall at the shoulder
Description: The Australian Cattle Dog, also known as a Blue Heeler or Red Heeler, depending on their coat color, is an extremely intelligent, active, and sturdy dog breed. These dogs are fast! And we’re talking  fast , fast! Originally developed by Australian settlers to handle herds of cattle on expansive ranches, they are still used as herding dogs today. These dogs thrive on having a job and being part of all family activities. They are loyal and protective of their families, though wary of outsiders. Besides herding work, these dogs do well at canine sports, including agility, obedience, rally, flyball, and flying disc competitions. Novice pet parents and apartment dwellers should beware: these dogs need a lot of mental and physical activity to stay happy and healthy and to prevent bored, destructive behavior. If you have a home with plenty of room to run and you can keep up with such an active pup, the Australian Cattle Dog may be the breed for you! When considering a Heeler puppy, it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, choosing a reputable breeder is crucial if you’re looking for Red or Blue Heeler puppies and decide to purchase them. Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable Australian Cattle Dog breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices. Intelligent, devoted, and fiercely loyal, these dogs are excellent family companions. Despite their strength, they are not prone to aggression and can seamlessly integrate into households, particularly those with children. However, early training may be required to address nipping tendencies, as their herding instincts may lead them to try herding children—an attribute shared with other herding breeds like  Border Collies . Positive reinforcement training techniques are effective in managing this behavior.

The Australian Kelpie, often referred to as the Kelpie, is a highly regarded and versatile herding  dog breed . As their name would indicate, these herding dogs hail from Australia. Breed enthusiasts prize these hardworking dogs for their agility, intelligence, and natural instinct to control livestock. While similar to the  Working Kelpie , the Australian Kelpie is classified as a separate breed. Characterized by their sleek and athletic appearance, Australian Kelpies are highly energetic and require plenty of physical and mental stimulation. They excel in dog sports, obedience training, and various canine activities, making them suitable for active families and farmers alike. Kelpies are also renowned for their strong bond with their owners, loyalty, and adaptability. With their remarkable herding abilities, intelligence, and loving nature, Australian Kelpies continue to be a treasured breed. When considering an Australian Kelpie, it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you decide to purchase an  Australian Kelpie puppy , it’s crucial to choose a  reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable Australian Kelpie breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures that you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

The Australian Retriever is a mixed breed dog — a cross between the  Australian Shepherd  and  Golden Retriever  dog breeds. Loyal, intelligent, and friendly, these pups inherited some of the best traits from both of their parents. If you’re looking for a devoted family dog who’s also smarter than your average pooch, an Australian Retriever  puppy  could be a perfect addition to your household. The Australian Shepherd Golden Retriever mix loves children and is quick to form long-lasting and loving bonds with the humans in their life. Just be aware that the Australian Shepherd Golden Retriever mix is one of the most energetic dogs around — so you’ll need to be able to commit to providing lots of  exercise  and play time and have a suitably spacious living arrangement. When considering an Australian Retriever, it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you decide to purchase, it’s crucial to choose a  reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable Australian Retriever breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures that you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

Height: 18 to 23 inches tall at the shoulder
Description: The Australian Shepherd is a medium-sized herding breed from the United States known for their colorful coats, intelligence, and friendly personality. Although the breed originated in the United States, their name reflects their ancestral roots, which are traced back to Europe via Australia. Initially utilized as herding dogs, Aussies still possess strong herding instincts today. American ranchers favored Australian Shepherds for their exceptional herding capabilities, and their popularity soared when they began appearing in rodeos. The Aussie temperament is energetic, loyal, sociable, affectionate, alert, and devoted. They are generally eager to please and easy to train, making them excellent companions for families with children and other pets. Due to their high activity levels, Aussies require ample exercise and thrive in various activities such as agility, obedience, and herding. Because of their high intelligence and intensity, they are not recommended for first-time or novice dog owners. As with any dog, early socialization is essential. When considering an Australian Shepherd, it’s advisable to prioritize adopting rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you’re  searching for Australian Shepherd puppies and decide to purchase one, choosing a  reputable breeder is crucial. Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable Australian Shepherd breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures that you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

The Australian Shepherd Husky is a mixed breed dog — a cross between the  Australian Shepherd  and  Siberian Husky  dog breeds. These active dogs are protective and loyal and inherited some of the best traits from both of their parents. The Australian Shepherd Husky is also called  Aussie Siberian . Despite their unfortunate status as a designer breed, you can find these mixed breed dogs in shelters and breed specific rescues, so remember to adopt! Don’t shop! These pups would make suitable companions for those who enjoy hiking and, in general, the great outdoors. They come from a long line of working dog parents and thrive in wide open spaces where they can exert some of their energy. Active people, hikers, and naturists, please step to the front of the line if you’re considering adopting one of these pups. See below for all Australian Shepherd Husky mixed dog breed facts and characteristics!

The Australian Shepherd Lab Mix is a mixed breed dog — a cross between the  Australian Shepherd  and the  Labrador Retriever  dog breeds. Medium in size, energetic, and loyal, these pups inherited some amazing traits from both of their parents. Australian Shepherd Lab Mixes are also called  Aussiedors , Australian Shepradors, Aussie Shepradors, Aussie Labs, and Shepradors. Despite their unfortunate status as a designer breed, you can find these mixed breed dogs in shelters and breed specific rescues, so adopt! Don’t shop! These athletic, alert pups are best suited for level two dog parents, so some dog parenting experience is needed. These pups do not do well with home bodies. They like active people and would really like their humans to put the mixed breed’s intelligence to use. Give them a job to do and watch them excel! See below for all mixed dog breed traits and facts about Australian Shepherd Lab Mixes!

The Australian Shepherd Pit Bull Mix is a mixed breed dog — a cross between the  Australian Shepherd  and  Pit Bull  dog breeds. Energetic, loyal, and quick to learn new things, these pups inherited some of the best traits from both of their parents. Australian Shepherd Pit Bull Mixes are sometimes also called Pitbull Australian Shepherd Mixes or Aussie Pits. Despite their unfortunate status as a designer breed, you can find these mixed breed dogs in shelters and breed specific rescues, so remember to adopt! Don’t shop! These adorable pups make great family dogs for larger households, although they can thrive in smaller, urban environments with active owners. If you want a playful dog who will be both a snuggle buddy and an alert system, the Austrian Shepherd Pit Bull Mix might be the right dog for you! See below for all mixed dog breed traits and facts about Australian Shepherd Pit Bull Mixes!

The Australian Stumpy Tail Cattle Dog, named for their characteristic short or sometimes non-existent tail, is a descendant of Australian wild dingoes and domesticated  herding dogs  from the late 19th century. Although similar to the popular  Australian Cattle Dog , the Stumpy is leaner, tailless, and more alert when it comes to strangers and new situations. Additionally, a true Stumpy will have no tan markings on their bodies. This active and intelligent breed has a lot of energy and requires a lot of space to burn it off. The Australian Stumpy Tail Cattle Dog is best suited for adult households or homes with older children, ideally with some fenced yard space to run around. If you want an active companion and have patience for consistent training, then this might be the breed for you! When considering  an Australian Stumpy Tail Cattle Dog puppy , it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you decide to purchase, it’s crucial to choose  a reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable Australian Stumpy Tail Cattle Dog breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures that you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

Height: 10 to 11 inches tall at the shoulder
Description: The Australian Terrier, a small and spirited breed, hails from Australia and is characterized by its distinctive rough double coat that comes in shades of blue and tan. Despite its diminutive size, this terrier is known for its confident and feisty personality. It’s an alert and vigilant watchdog, always ready to alert its family to any potential threats. Australian Terriers are also remarkably loyal and affectionate with their owners, making them loving companions. Their intelligence and curiosity make them quick learners, but their independent streak may require consistent training. With their compact size and adaptable nature, Australian Terriers are suitable for both city and country living, provided they receive regular exercise and mental stimulation. Despite their vitality, their compact size and low-shedding, low-maintenance coat make them suitable for active apartment dwellers. If you desire a reliable watchdog, this breed’s loyalty and inclination to alert their owners to unusual occurrences might meet your requirements. By fulfilling their needs, you can ensure a lifelong bond with a loving and devoted little friend. When considering an Australian Terrier, it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from  rescue organizations  or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you decide to purchase an  Australian Terrier puppy , it’s crucial to choose a  reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable Australian Terrier breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures that you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

Height: 23 to 27 inches tall at the shoulder
Description: The Azawakh is a sighthound breed originating from West Africa. These elegant and athletic dogs are known for their impressive speed and agility. The nomadic Tuareg people of the Sahara desert historically bred them for hunting game, including gazelles and other swift prey. Today, the Azawakh continues to excel in various  dog sports  and is gaining popularity as a companion and show dog. These remarkable dogs have a statuesque build, with a deep chest and long, slender legs that enable them to cover vast distances with ease. They stand about 23 to 29 inches tall at the shoulder and weigh between 35 to 55 pounds. Their short coat further enhances their distinctive appearance, coming in various colors and patterns, such as red, blue, black, brindle, and white. These dogs have a gentle and loyal nature, forming strong bonds with their families. On the other hand, they have a known reputation for their independent and aloof demeanor towards strangers, which makes them excellent watchdogs. Early  socialization  is crucial to ensure that they remain well-adjusted and confident in various situations. It’s important to note that training Azawakhs can be a bit of a challenge due to their independent nature and high energy levels. For any future pet parents of an  Azawakh puppy , positive reinforcement methods work best, and consistency is key. They respond well to praise and rewards, making training a rewarding experience for both the dog and the owner. When considering a Azawakh, it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you’re on the search for Azawakh puppies and decide to purchase, it’s crucial to choose a reputable breeder. Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable Azawakh breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures that you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

Height: 20 to 25 inches tall at the shoulder
Description: The Barbet, also known as the French Water Dog, is a medium-sized sporting breed first bred to retrieve waterfowl. They possess a distinctive, hypoallergenic curly coat in multiple colors, including grey, black, fawn, or brown, usually with white markings. Their fuzzy coat often hangs over their eyes, giving them the impression of an oversized teddy bear. In addition to their unique coat, the Barbet’s physical characteristics include webbed feet and a well-muscled body to help them easily move through the water. While avid swimmers, they are also talented agility competitors in their native France. The Barbet’s temperament is intelligent, playful, energetic, and eager to please. As a result, they are easy to train, provided you can keep sessions interesting and reward progress with a high-quality treat. Barbets are also sociable and friendly, making them excellent companions for families and individuals. This devoted breed is also compatible with children and other dogs. The Barbet makes a wonderful companion in homes where their needs, including proper exercise and mental stimulation, can be met. When considering a Barbet, it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you decide to purchase a  Barbet puppy , it’s crucial to choose  a reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable Barbet breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures that you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

Height: 16 to 17 inches tall at the shoulder
Description: The Basenji is an ancient hunting dog originally from Central Africa and categorized as both a sighthound and a scenthound. They stand between 16 and 24 inches tall at the shoulder and weigh between 22 and 24 pounds. Their coat is short and smooth and is typically red, black, brindle, or fawn. Basenjis have a long, narrow head with a pointed muzzle and erect ears. They have a deep chest and a short, muscular body. Basenjis are known for making unique barkless vocalizations. They yodel, which is a high-pitched, somewhat musical sound. They also bark but are much quieter than other dogs. This is a high-energy dog that requires a lot of exercise.  They need at least 30 minutes of exercise per day, preferably more. Basenjis are also very intelligent dogs that need mental stimulation. Because of their active mind, they are often good at agility and obedience training. While highly intelligent, their independence can lead to stubborn streaks. This breed requires an experienced pet owner, patience, and consistent training. This may be worth considering for any first-time pet parents considering adding a Basenji puppy to their home. But this breed can be a loyal companion with time, dedication, and patience. When considering a Basenji, it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you decide to purchase a Basenji dog, it’s crucial to choose a  reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable Basenji breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

The Bassador is a mixed breed dog–a cross between the  Basset Hound  and the  Labrador Retriever  dog breeds. Loyal, sometimes stubborn, and always sniffing around, these pups inherited some of the best traits from both of their parents. Bassadors go by several names, including Basset Lab, Basador, and sometimes even Bassetdor. Despite their status as a designer breed, you may find these mixed breed dogs in shelters and rescues, so remember to adopt! Don’t shop! Bassadors come from two very different parents, so their perfect living situation will depend on the individual dog. In general, Bassadors love attention, especially from their favorite person, and will need to live in a place with plenty of space to roam. If you want a dog with a very unique look who’s loyal to a fault, the Bassador could be the dog for you! See below for all mixed dog breed traits and facts about Bassadors!

The Basset Fauve de Bretagne dog, originating from France, is an old  breed  with a unique appearance and historical significance. With a lineage tracing back to the 19th century, these dogs were selectively bred from larger French hounds to create a compact yet robust hunter, specifically tailored for navigating the challenging terrains of Brittany. Known for their keen sense of smell, they were originally employed for hunting small game, showcasing determination and agility in their pursuit. With a wiry, fawn-colored coat and expressive, soulful eyes, Basset Fauve de Bretagne charm both hunters and families alike. Their muscular build and long ears reflect their original purpose as adept hunters. Despite their hunting heritage, these dogs have evolved into versatile companions with an adaptable nature, thriving in various living environments. Today, they bring a rich blend of history, utility, and companionship to their homes. When considering a  Basset Fauve de Bretagne puppy , it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you decide to purchase, it’s crucial to choose  a reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable Basset Fauve de Bretagne puppy breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures that you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

Height: Up to 14 inches tall at the shoulder
Description: The Basset Hound, originally bred in France as a  hunting companion , is a medium-sized  hound dog  with a long, low body and short legs. They are known for their distinctive features, including their long, droopy ears, wrinkled foreheads, and sad-looking eyes. These gentle and affectionate dogs make great family pets. They are also relatively low-maintenance, requiring only moderate exercise. Basset Hounds are known for their laid-back personalities. They are not as active as some other breeds, but they do require regular exercise. A daily walk or hike is sufficient to keep this pup healthy and happy. As a hound dog, they are also known for their stubbornness. Like other hound dogs, they can be difficult to train, but with patience and consistency, they can learn basic commands. When considering a Basset Hound, it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you’re on the search for  Basset Hound puppies , it’s crucial to choose a  reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable Basset Hound breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures that you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

The Basset Retriever is a medium-sized cross between the  Basset Hound  and  Golden Retriever  dog breeds. With a friendly, affectionate, and intelligent temperament, Basset Retrievers inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents. You can find these mixed-breed dogs in shelters and breed-specific rescues, so remember to always adopt! The Basset Retriever is a great family dog with a mild-mannered disposition, a willingness to please, and a loving and devoted nature. This mixed breed is extra friendly and gets along well with children. However, due to their hunting heritage, they will require much exercise to stay healthy and happy. These dogs do best with active and busy families. When considering a Basset Retriever, it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a needy dog. However, if you decide to purchase a Basset Retriever puppy, it’s crucial to choose  a reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

The Bavarian Mountain Scent Hound, also known as the Bavarian Mountain Hound, originated in Germany. Developed in the 19th century, this stunning breed came from the Bavaria region in the southeast of Germany. Breeders specifically bred this dog for its exceptional scenting abilities, making it an ideal  companion for tracking and hunting  in challenging mountain terrains. The Bavarian Mountain Scent Hound boasts a well-proportioned, medium-sized body with a robust and muscular build. This breed’s physique helps it navigate through rugged landscapes with ease. The most common colors for the short, dense coat are brindle, reddish brown, and tan. However, fawn colors, red, and a few other colors are also possible. These colors not only give the breed a striking appearance but also aid in camouflage while hunting. This breed’s highly developed  sense of smell  makes it particularly revered. Equipped with keen noses, they excel at seeking game such as deer, boar, and other large animals found in mountainous regions. These versatile dogs are intelligent and eager to please, which can make training a  Bavarian Scent Hound Puppy  fairly easy. When considering a Bavarian Mountain Scent Hound, it’s advisable to prioritize adopting from rescue organizations or shelters to provide a loving home to a dog in need. However, if you decide to purchase, it’s crucial to choose a  reputable breeder . Conduct thorough research to ensure that the breeder follows ethical practices and prioritizes the well-being of their dogs. Reputable Bavarian Mountain Scent Hound breeders prioritize the health and temperament of their dogs, conduct necessary health screenings, and provide a nurturing environment for the puppies. This active approach ensures that you bring home a healthy and happy pup while discouraging unethical breeding practices.

The Beabull is a medium-sized designer cross between the  Beagle  and the  English Bulldog  breeds. Loyal, curious, and loving, these pups inherited some of the best qualities from both of their parents. The Beabull doesn’t go by many other names, though some people might just call them Beagle-Bulldog mixes. These cute pups are quite versatile and can adapt to both apartment living or a home with a backyard to run in. Though their short, stout body types may lead you to believe they are lazy, these dogs will get bursts of energy and enjoy a game of fetch or a trip to the dog park. The Beabull bonds well with their owner and every member of the family, and they’re fit for either single- or multi-person family households. These loving pups are protective and playful, and they can be the perfect companion for you. The Beabull, a mix between a Beagle and an English Bulldog, typically sports a short, smooth coat that is easy to maintain. Common colors include white, brown, black, fawn, and brindle, often combined in unique patches or patterns. Some Beabulls inherit the distinctive wrinkled skin of the Bulldog, while others may feature the tri-color pattern of the Beagle.
